title: Webhooks Integration
summary: Webhooks integration covering HMAC signature validation for GitHub/GitLab/generic sources, route configuration with prompt templates, direct delivery mode (deliver_only, zero LLM tokens), dynamic subscriptions, rate limiting, and idempotency via event ID deduplication.
key_concepts: Webhook routes: map incoming webhooks to Hermes sessions
HMAC signature validation: GitHub (sha256), GitLab (X-Gitlab-Token), generic
Prompt templates with webhook payload variable substitution
Direct delivery mode (deliver_only): forward payloads without LLM processing
Dynamic subscriptions: POST /webhooks/subscribe to add routes at runtime
Payload filtering: only process events matching field conditions
Outgoing webhooks: Hermes can POST to external URLs on events
Multi-destination fan-out: one inbound webhook triggers multiple sessions

env_vars.name: WEBHOOK_SECRET
env_vars.description: Default HMAC secret for generic webhook signature validation

env_vars.name: WEBHOOK_BASE_URL
env_vars.description: Public base URL for webhook endpoints (used in subscription responses)

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway; webhook routes are registered at /webhooks/<route_name>
Dynamically register a new webhook route at runtime

config_keys.description: Signature type: github, gitlab, generic (default: generic)
Jinja2 template for LLM prompt; payload fields available as variables
Forward payload to session without LLM processing (zero tokens)
Max requests per minute for this route (default: no limit)
Field conditions to filter which payloads are processed
Session IDs or platform channels to fan-out to

code_examples.title: GitHub webhook route
code_examples.code:
webhooks:
  routes:
    - name: github-pr
      secret: "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
      signature_type: github
      filter:
        action: ["opened", "synchronize", "closed"]
      prompt_template: |
        GitHub PR #{{ number }} {{ action }}:
        Title: {{ pull_request.title }}
        Author: {{ pull_request.user.login }}
        URL: {{ pull_request.html_url }}
        Summarize this PR event and suggest any action items.

code_examples.title: Direct delivery mode (zero LLM tokens)
code_examples.code:
webhooks:
  routes:
    - name: monitoring-alerts
      secret: "${ALERT_SECRET}"
      deliver_only: true  # payload forwarded as-is, no LLM call
      destinations:
        - "telegram:@myusername"

code_examples.title: GitLab webhook route
code_examples.code:
webhooks:
  routes:
    - name: gitlab-ci
      secret: "${GITLAB_TOKEN}"
      signature_type: gitlab
      filter:
        object_kind: ["pipeline"]
      prompt_template: "GitLab pipeline {{ object_attributes.status }} for {{ project.name }}. Analyze."

code_examples.title: Rate limiting
code_examples.code:
webhooks:
  routes:
    - name: noisy-sensor
      secret: "${SENSOR_SECRET}"
      rate_limit: 10  # max 10 requests/minute; extras dropped

gotchas: deliver_only routes forward the raw JSON payload to the destination session; the payload appears as the "user message" — no LLM call is made and no tokens are consumed
HMAC signature validation order: github uses sha256 with "sha256=" prefix; gitlab uses plain token in X-Gitlab-Token header; generic uses sha256 without prefix
Prompt templates use Jinja2 syntax; access nested payload fields with dot notation ({{ pull_request.user.login }})
filter conditions use exact string matching or array membership; complex filtering (regex, range) requires a custom route handler
Idempotency deduplication checks X-Webhook-ID or X-GitHub-Delivery headers; duplicate event IDs within 5 minutes are dropped
Dynamic subscriptions via POST /webhooks/subscribe are not persisted; routes are lost on gateway restart unless also in config
Multi-destination fan-out sends the same prompt to each destination in parallel; ensure each destination has an active session
full_text:
## Webhooks

Receive webhooks from external services (GitHub, GitLab, monitoring tools, IoT devices, etc.) and route them to Hermes sessions.

### Route configuration

```yaml
webhooks:
  routes:
    - name: github-pr
      secret: "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
      signature_type: github
      filter:
        action: ["opened", "synchronize", "closed"]
      prompt_template: |
        GitHub PR #{{ number }} {{ action }}:
        Title: {{ pull_request.title }}
        Author: {{ pull_request.user.login }}
        Summarize and suggest action items.
```

Each route registers an endpoint at `/webhooks/<name>`.

### Signature types

| Type | Header | Format |
|------|--------|--------|
| `github` | `X-Hub-Signature-256` | `sha256=<hex>` |
| `gitlab` | `X-Gitlab-Token` | plain token |
| `generic` | `X-Webhook-Signature` | `sha256=<hex>` |

### Prompt templates

Templates use Jinja2 syntax. Access any webhook payload field as a variable:

```
{{ pull_request.title }}      — nested field
{{ action }}                  — top-level field
{{ commits | length }}        — Jinja2 filter
```

### Direct delivery mode

For high-frequency events where you want zero LLM processing:

```yaml
- name: monitoring-alerts
  deliver_only: true
  destinations:
    - "telegram:@myusername"
```

The raw JSON payload is forwarded to each destination session as the message body. No LLM call, no token cost.

### Payload filtering

Only process events matching specific field values:

```yaml
filter:
  action: ["opened", "closed"]   # array = "any of"
  object_kind: pipeline          # string = exact match
```

Events not matching the filter are acknowledged (HTTP 200) but silently dropped.

### Rate limiting

```yaml
- name: noisy-sensor
  rate_limit: 10  # max 10 requests/minute
```

Excess requests receive HTTP 429.

### Idempotency

Hermes deduplicates on `X-GitHub-Delivery` or `X-Webhook-ID` headers. Duplicate event IDs within 5 minutes are acknowledged but not processed.

### Dynamic subscriptions

Register routes at runtime without restarting the gateway:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/webhooks/subscribe \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "my-hook", "secret": "mysecret", "destination": "telegram:@myusername"}'
```

**Note:** Dynamic routes are in-memory only. Persist them in config for survival across restarts.

### Fan-out

One inbound webhook can trigger multiple destinations:

```yaml
- name: alerts
  destinations:
    - "telegram:@myusername"
    - "slack:C0123456789"
    - "discord:9876543210"
```

All destinations receive the same processed response in parallel.
